title: delphinium virescens subsp. penardii
intro: Delphinium virescens subsp. penardii, commonly known as the alpine delphinium or penard's delphinium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Delphinium virescens subsp. penardii have been found to provide several health benefits, including relieving coughs, reducing fever, and soothing bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
